name: Abarán and Cerro de la Atalaya Circular
name_original: Abarán a La Cruz de Abarán a Centro de visitantes de Siyâsa — circular
description: This difficult 13.8-mile (22.2 km) circular jogging route from Abarán offers river views, orchards, and historical sites.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Abarán traverse a varied landscape shaped by the Segura River, flanked by the Sierra del Oro and Sierra de la Pila mountain ranges. The region features a verdant riverine environment with promenades and natural parks, contrasting with the more challenging terrain of the surrounding hills. Traditional orchards and agricultural areas also form part of the scenic backdrop for running. Best jogging routes around Abarán The most popular jogging route is Abarán and Cerro de la Atalaya Circular, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views after a challenging climb. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra del Oro and Atalaya Loop, a difficult 14.2 miles (22.9 km) path. This route explores the rugged terrain of the Sierra del Oro, providing extensive mountain vistas. Local runners also love the Medina Siyâsa Trail, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Jogging around Abarán is defined by riverside paths along the Segura River, climbs into the Sierra del Oro, and routes through traditional orchards. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Abarán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Abarán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I explore around Abarán?
A: Abarán offers a diverse network of over 49 running routes. These paths traverse varied landscapes, from riverside promenades to challenging mountain trails, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Abarán?
A: While many routes in Abarán are classified as moderate to difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375226917/playa-fluvial-la-presa-desde-cieza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa fluvial "La Presa" desde Cieza — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that offers a relatively gentle experience. Additionally, the local &lt;a href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Norias&lt;/a&gt;, though not listed as a komoot tour here, is described as having a low difficulty level, making it accessible for all fitness levels and ideal for a refreshing jog through parks, orchards, and along the river.

Q: What are some longer distance running trails for advanced runners?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Abarán provides several longer and more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371409359/sierra-del-oro-and-atalaya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Oro and Atalaya Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.2-mile (22.9 km) path exploring rugged mountain terrain. Another excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375846489/abaran-and-cerro-de-la-atalaya-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abarán and Cerro de la Atalaya Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.8-mile (22.2 km) route offering panoramic views after a significant climb.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in the Abarán area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Abarán are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375846489/abaran-and-cerro-de-la-atalaya-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abarán and Cerro de la Atalaya Circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375226917/playa-fluvial-la-presa-desde-cieza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa fluvial "La Presa" desde Cieza — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient loop experiences.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes and attractions can I expect to see while running in Abarán?
A: Jogging in Abarán offers a rich tapestry of sights. You'll encounter the verdant Segura River and its picturesque promenades, traditional orchards bursting with peach, lemon, and orange trees, and the dramatic backdrops of the Sierra del Oro and Sierra de la Pila mountain ranges. Key attractions include the historic waterwheels along the Ruta de las Norias, such as the impressive Noria Grande, and viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in Abarán?
A: For families, the riverside areas and parks offer pleasant environments for a jog. The &lt;a href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Norias&lt;/a&gt; is particularly suitable, as it's a low-difficulty, circular trail passing through parks and orchards, showcasing the region's unique water heritage. The El Jarral Natural Paradise also provides a peaceful riverside setting perfect for spending time outdoors.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Abarán?
A: Abarán's warm local climate makes it suitable for jogging year-round. However, spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for outdoor activities. In spring, the orchards are vibrant with blooms, offering a sensory experience with the scent of citrus. Summer mornings or evenings are best to avoid the midday heat, while winter provides cooler, refreshing conditions.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or summits accessible from the running trails?
A: Absolutely. Many routes lead to rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán&lt;/a&gt;, accessible after a challenging climb, offers panoramic views of the Sierra del Oro and the valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3965357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Pelado Shelter&lt;/a&gt; also provides incredible vistas towards the entire Sierra de la Pila, making for a rewarding destination on a run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Abarán?
A: The running routes in Abarán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from scenic riverside paths along the Segura River to challenging climbs into the Sierra del Oro, and the unique experience of running through traditional orchards.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments or unique features to look out for?
A: Yes, Abarán is rich in natural and historical features. Along your runs, you might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5051429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient irrigation channel, or the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2760010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortao de las Peñas&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. The region is also home to the Noria Grande, Europe's largest functioning waterwheel, a testament to the area's ingenious hydraulic heritage.

Q: Can I find routes that combine running with exploring historical sites?
A: Many routes in Abarán naturally blend outdoor activity with cultural exploration. The &lt;a href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Norias&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, allowing you to jog past several traditional waterwheels and the historic Puente Viejo (Old Bridge). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375215105/medina-siyasa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Siyâsa Trail&lt;/a&gt; also leads through varied landscapes that often include historical elements of the region.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a chance for wild swimming nearby?
A: While not directly on the running routes, the Segura River in Abarán is known for its free-swimming areas, particularly in the El Jarral Natural Paradise. This area features a fluvial beach and a peaceful natural retreat, making it a popular spot for adventure sports and a refreshing dip after a run.
tips: Beautiful place to enjoy spectacular panoramic views after an entertaining climb up the Senda del Monje, the only way to get there passing through the Sanctuary of the Virgen de Oro.

The Virgen del Buen Suceso is the patron saint of Cieza and in 1962 they built a small hermitage in her honor in the upper part of the Watchtower. There is a viewpoint with a cross and the views from here are spectacular. In September they take the Virgin to the Church of the Assumption and a week later they take her back to her hermitage.

You cannot miss this archaeological site, one of the most important of its kind in Europe.
Located in a strategic point on the Segura River valley, it was discovered in the '80s, the excavations brought to light 19 houses and an invaluable collection of objects (ceramics, glass and plaster architectural elements).

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Abarán to La Cruz de Abarán to Centro de visitantes de Siyâsa circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for this specific 22 km route isn't detailed, many trails in the area, including those leading to the Siyâsa archaeological site, often start near the Molino de Teodoro in Cieza. Parking would likely be available in Cieza, a short distance from Abarán, or at designated spots closer to the Siyâsa Visitor Center.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife or other trail users. Be prepared to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The spring and autumn months are ideal for jogging this route. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery, especially the orchards along the Segura river, is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs necessary, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the general trail network in this area for jogging. However, if you plan to visit the Siyâsa Visitor Center or the archaeological site itself, there might be specific entry fees or guided tour requirements.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse terrain. You can expect sections of asphalt or cemented paths, especially near urban areas or riverbanks, transitioning into rural and forested paths. There are also more challenging, uneven sections, such as the 'Sarraceno' path, which involves a steep climb. Sensible footwear with good grip is highly recommended.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Abarán to La Cruz de Abarán to Centro de visitantes de Siyâsa route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Highlights include the Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán, the Viewpoint of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Buen Suceso, and the Siyâsa Visitor Center. The trail also offers views of the Segura river, traditional fruit orchards, the impressive Atalaya rock formation, and glimpses of the historic urban landscape of Cieza.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Transmurciana 22, Camino de Santiago del Azahar, Río Segura, and Medina Siyasa (Cieza) trails, among others. This offers opportunities to explore different segments or extend your run.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: As indicated by its name, "Abarán to La Cruz de Abarán to Centro de visitantes de Siyâsa — circular," this is a loop trail. You will start and finish at the same general location, making it convenient for parking and logistics.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length (over 22 km) and 'difficult' rating, it's essential to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Appropriate trail running shoes are a must due to the varied and sometimes uneven terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and snacks are also highly recommended for this extended effort.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the Siyâsa Visitor Center after jogging?
faqs.answer: The Siyâsa Visitor Center is located near Cieza, which offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and shops in Cieza where you can refuel and relax after your run.

faqs.question: Is the Abarán to La Cruz de Abarán to Centro de visitantes de Siyâsa route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of over 22 kilometers and a 'difficult' rating with significant elevation gain (nearly 700 meters), this specific route is generally not recommended for beginner joggers. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with varied terrain. Beginners might consider shorter, less challenging sections of trails in the area.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán
Viewpoint of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Buen Suceso
